question.text: Scenario: Mr. Harish Gupta, aged 50, is a business owner with a net worth of Rs. 8 crore. He has Rs. 1 crore available for equity investment and is evaluating two options: Option A — Equity Mutual Fund (Direct Plan, Growth): Large & Mid-Cap Fund with expense ratio 0.75%, SEBI-mandated diversification (minimum 35% large-cap, 35% mid-cap), no minimum investment, exit load 1% if redeemed within 1 year, daily NAV disclosure, SEBI Mutual Fund Regulations apply. Option B — Discretionary Equity PMS: Minimum investment Rs. 50 lakh, fixed management fee 1.5% p.a., performance fee 20% of profits above 10% hurdle rate (no catch-up, high watermark applies), portfolio of 15-25 stocks customized to Harish's risk profile, Rs. 5 crore net worth of PMS provider, quarterly portfolio disclosure. Additional information: Harish is in the 30% income tax bracket. He expects a 5-year investment horizon. He wants concentrated, high-conviction stock picking rather than broad diversification. He has a separate emergency fund and does not need liquidity from this investment. Question 4 of 5: If Harish invests Rs. 1 crore in the mutual fund and it grows to Rs. 1.5 crore in 2 years, what is his LTCG tax liability upon full redemption?

answer.text: Rs. 4,68,750 (12.5% on Rs. 50L gain minus Rs. 1.25L exemption, i.e., 12.5% of Rs. 48.75L, which is Rs. 6,09,375, minus... )

explanation.text: **The Logic:** LTCG = Rs. 1.5 crore - Rs. 1 crore = Rs. 50 lakh. Exempt: Rs. 1.25 lakh. Taxable LTCG = Rs. 50L - Rs. 1.25L = Rs. 48.75 lakh. Tax = 12.5% × Rs. 48,75,000 = Rs. 6,09,375 (plus applicable surcharge and cess). **The Trap:** The Rs. 1.25 lakh exemption must be deducted before applying the 12.5% rate. Forgetting this exemption overstates the tax. **Cross-Exam Context:** Equity MF LTCG tax calculation is tested in V-A and X-A with current rates. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** If Harish redeems Rs. 50 lakh each year over 2 years, he can use Rs. 1.25 lakh exemption twice — potentially saving Rs. 15,625 in tax. Staggered redemption is more tax-efficient.
